chunks.page_summary: This page provides an assembly guide and overview of Rheonics Teletube Sensors, detailing their modular design for long insertion length installations and various components.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412

rheonics

About Rheonics Teletube Sensors

For installations where the insertion length required for the Rheonics SRV or SRD sensors is ultra-
long (above 600mm), Rheonics offers the Teletube sensor variants. This variant allows the unit
to be delivered in smaller parts that are assembled by the user prior to final installation on site.
Assembly is straightforward and simplified based on G-threaded joints. Sensor probes used for
this assembly are called Teletube probes (SR-TT), these probes have a ¾” G male thread at the
back. This variant simplifies the delivery and assembly, as well as reduces the costs of using a long
insertion standard probe.

For the Type SR-TT probes, the user should define the process connection, total length (if not
defined, the smallest possible is used), insertion length (distance from probe's tip to flange), and
the back-end variant. The back-end variant can be -TR for SME-TR(D) mounting adapter or -CAB
for a metal Cable Gland.

SME-TR or -TR

AT-TT

SRx-TT-TR

SRx-TT-L

EXT-TT

OR-TT

SRx-TT

SRx-TT-LAB

CG-TT
Cable gland

OR-TT

AB-TT

Figure 1: Rheonics Teletube Sensor Components

Note: The Type SR-TT probes can also be ordered alone without extension tubes or process
connection flanges. In this case, the client needs to create a custom pipe to thread at the
probe's back end. Using the AT-TT on the probe back end, the custom pipe requires a standard
1” NPT female port for mounting.

Contact Rheonics Support Team for more information at support@rheonics.com.

3
key_facts: Rheonics Teletube sensor variants are designed for installations requiring ultra-long insertion lengths (above 600mm).
Teletube sensors are delivered in smaller, user-assembled parts to simplify delivery and assembly and reduce costs.
Assembly of Teletube sensors is straightforward and uses G-threaded joints.
Teletube probes (SR-TT) have a ¾” G male thread at the back.
Users must define the process connection, total length, insertion length, and back-end variant for SR-TT probes.
The back-end variant can be -TR for SME-TR(D) mounting adapter or -CAB for a metal Cable Gland.
SR-TT probes can be ordered without extension tubes or process connection flanges.
When using an AT-TT on the probe back end for a custom pipe, the pipe needs a standard 1” NPT female port for mounting.
warnings_notes: Note: The Type SR-TT probes can also be ordered alone without extension tubes or process connection flanges. In this case, the client needs to create a custom pipe to thread at the probe's back end. Using the AT-TT on the probe back end, the custom pipe requires a standard 1” NPT female port for mounting.

chunks.page_summary: This page from the Rheonics SR-Teletube Assembly Guide provides a parts list for the Teletube Sensor Probe, detailing each component's quantity, description, and an associated image.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412
rheonics
Rheonics Teletube Sensor Probe - Parts List
The next table details all parts that can be used for the assembly, depending on the sensor configuration some parts may not be included and quantities can vary.

Part | Quantity | Comment | Images
SRV-TT or SRD-TT | 1 | Sensor Probe
OR-TT | 1 for Probe
1 for Back-end connector
1 for each extension tube | Ø27X1.5 O-ring. Select the right material for your process fluid and operational temperature compatibility.
EXT-TT | Defined by insertion length | Extension tubes: May be one or many. These parts are numbered from 1 to X. The part with the process connection flange is generally the last one.
AB-TT | 1 | End back adapter Female G3/4" - Female NPT 1/2". Only for -TT-CAB option.
CG-TT | 1 | Only for -TT-CAB option (Cable gland for sensor cable)
AT-TT | 1 | End back adapter Female G3/4" - Male NPT 1/2”. Only for -TT-TR option (Transmitter mount adapter).
4
key_facts: The document is part of the SR-Teletube Assembly Guide.
The table lists all parts that can be used for the Rheonics Teletube Sensor Probe assembly.
SRV-TT or SRD-TT refers to the Sensor Probe itself, typically supplied as 1 unit.
OR-TT O-rings are used for the probe, back-end connector, and each extension tube.
EXT-TT refers to extension tubes, whose quantity is defined by the required insertion length.
AB-TT is an end back adapter with Female G3/4" and Female NPT 1/2" threads, used exclusively with the -TT-CAB option.
CG-TT is a cable gland for the sensor cable, specifically for the -TT-CAB option.
AT-TT is an end back adapter with Female G3/4" and Male NPT 1/2" threads, used exclusively as a transmitter mount adapter for the -TT-TR option.
warnings_notes: Depending on the sensor configuration, some parts may not be included and quantities can vary.
For OR-TT O-rings, select the right material for your process fluid and operational temperature compatibility.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides essential information for the assembly of Rheonics Teletube sensors, focusing on O-ring selection, temperature considerations, and the use of included assembly tools like a torque wrench and thread locker glue.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412
rheonics

Note: The OR-TT is a standard O-Ring that is used for sealing all the G threaded in the Teletube assembly. This is available in various materials from Rheonics and third-party O-ring suppliers. Order the correct O-ring material that is compatible with your process fluid and operational temperature.

Follow the specified temperature for your probes as components are only rated for the temperature that was ordered. The use of probes outside of their specified operational range will result in irreversible damage.

Assembly Tools Included

| Tools   | Quantity | Comment                                        | Images       |
| :------ | :------- | :--------------------------------------------- | :----------- |
| TOW-M12 | 1        | Torque Wrench for M12 connector                | [Image]      |
| TLG     | 1        | Thread Locker Glue
           Standard: LOCTITE 242
           For High Temperatures: LOCTITE 2422 HI TEMP TL MS 30G | [Image]      |

Note: It is recommended to use TLG (Thread Locker Glue) to avoid the threads coming loose. However, when the sensor needs to be disassembled, then you can assemble the Teletube sensor without the TLG and only apply TLG when the permanent configuration is fixed. TLG is reworkable in some cases - check the TLG is appropriate for your use case and process fluid.

For sensor rentals the TLG is not included as the installation is intended to be disassembled later.

5
key_facts: The OR-TT is a standard O-Ring designed for sealing G-threaded connections in the Teletube assembly.
OR-TT O-rings are available in various materials from Rheonics and third-party suppliers.
It is crucial to order the correct O-ring material that is compatible with the specific process fluid and operational temperature.
Probes are rated for a specific temperature, and operating them outside this range will cause irreversible damage.
Assembly tools included are a TOW-M12 Torque Wrench for M12 connectors and TLG (Thread Locker Glue).
The standard Thread Locker Glue is LOCTITE 242, with LOCTITE 2422 HI TEMP TL MS 30G available for high temperatures.
Using TLG is recommended to prevent threads from coming loose, but it may be omitted if the sensor needs future disassembly.
If the configuration is not permanent, TLG should only be applied once the final setup is fixed.
TLG is reworkable in some cases, but its suitability for a specific use case and process fluid should be verified.
TLG is not provided with sensor rentals because rental installations are designed for temporary use and later disassembly.
warnings_notes: Order the correct O-ring material that is compatible with your process fluid and operational temperature.
The use of probes outside of their specified operational range will result in irreversible damage.
It is recommended to use TLG (Thread Locker Glue) to avoid the threads coming loose.
When the sensor needs to be disassembled, then you can assemble the Teletube sensor without the TLG and only apply TLG when the permanent configuration is fixed.
TLG is reworkable in some cases - check the TLG is appropriate for your use case and process fluid.
For sensor rentals the TLG is not included as the installation is intended to be disassembled later.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides assembly instructions for Rheonics SR-Teletube extension tubes, detailing steps for preparing and connecting the tubes.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412
Assembly Instructions

N | Instruction | Drawings | Pictures

Verify all parts for assembly and tools to be used.

I. Prepare extension Tubes: If only one extension tube EXT-TT is delivered, no preparation is required, go to step 5. If multiple EXT-TT parts are delivered, follow next steps.

1 | Identify female and male G3/4" thread port of the EXT-TT part.
  | Identify the EXT-TT with flange, this will be the last tube to assemble - not applicable if APC adapter is used.

2 | Place an O-Ring OR-TT at the male thread port base.

3 | Apply some of the TLG paste in the male G3/4" threads for secure thread lock.
  | Not applicable for rentals.

6
key_facts: This document is an assembly guide for SR-Teletube extension tubes.
The document identifier is SR-TT-AG-2412.
Preparation of extension tubes depends on the number of EXT-TT parts delivered: single tubes require no preparation (go to step 5), while multiple tubes follow detailed steps.
Assembly involves identifying male and female G3/4" thread ports on EXT-TT parts.
An O-Ring (OR-TT) must be placed at the male thread port base.
TLG paste is used on male G3/4" threads for secure thread locking.
The EXT-TT part with a flange is the last tube to be assembled, unless an APC adapter is used.
warnings_notes: Identify the EXT-TT with flange, this will be the last tube to assemble - not applicable if APC adapter is used.
Applying TLG paste for thread lock is not applicable for rentals.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for assembling components of the SR-Teletube system, including threading male and female G3/4" ports, placing O-rings, applying paste, and handling specific connections for the -TT-CAB variant.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412
rheonics
4
Thread in the G3/4" male and female port until faces get in contact and O-Ring is compressed. Hand tightening is enough.
Repeat the process (step 2 to 4) for all EXT-TT delivered in the assigned order.
If there is a tube with a process connection, this one will be the last in the assembly.
5
Place an OR-TT on the G3/4" male port base of the last EXT-TT. Piece may look different depending on the process connection you ordered.
6
Apply some TLG paste in the male G3/4" threads.
Not applicable for rentals.
7A
A. For -TT-CAB only
Thread in the AB-TT G3/4" female port to the last EXT-TT G3/4" male port.
7
key_facts: Components should be hand-tightened until male and female port faces get in contact and the O-Ring is compressed.
The assembly process (steps 2 to 4) should be repeated for all EXT-TT components delivered in the assigned order.
If a tube with a process connection is part of the assembly, it will be the last component.
An OR-TT should be placed on the G3/4" male port base of the last EXT-TT.
TLG paste should be applied to the male G3/4" threads.
For -TT-CAB only, the AB-TT G3/4" female port should be threaded into the last EXT-TT G3/4" male port.
warnings_notes: Hand tightening is enough.
This step (applying TLG paste) is not applicable for rentals.
The piece may look different depending on the process connection ordered.
Step 7A is applicable only for -TT-CAB.

chunks.page_summary: This page details assembly steps for the SR-Teletube, including threading extension tubes, routing sensor cables, and placing an O-ring on the sensor probe.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412
rheonics
B. For -TT-TR only
7B
Thread in the AT-TT G3/4” female port to the last EXT-TT G3/4” male port
II. Sensor probe and cable
8
Insert the sensor cable through the assembled extension tube.
NOTE: If you used the AT-TT part the back-end will look different.
9
Place an OR-TT at the base of the G3/4" thread port of the sensor probe SRV-TT or SRD-TT.
8
key_facts: The AT-TT G3/4” female port should be threaded to the last EXT-TT G3/4” male port.
The sensor cable must be inserted through the assembled extension tube.
An OR-TT should be placed at the base of the G3/4" thread port of the SRV-TT or SRD-TT sensor probe.
warnings_notes: NOTE: If you used the AT-TT part the back-end will look different.

chunks.page_summary: This page from an assembly guide illustrates three steps (10-12) for connecting a sensor cable and an extension tube to a Rheonics viscometer/density sensor probe.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412

10
Thread in the sensor
cable M12 connector to
the M12 port at the
sensor probe back end.
Use the TOW-M12 tool
for a secure connection

11
Apply some TLG paste in
the male G3/4" threads
of the sensor probe.
Not applicable for
rentals.

12
Thread in the sensor
probe G3/4" port into
the extension tube
assembly.

9
key_facts: Step 10 involves threading the M12 connector of the sensor cable into the M12 port at the sensor probe's back end.
The TOW-M12 tool should be used for a secure connection in Step 10.
Step 11 instructs applying TLG paste to the male G3/4" threads of the sensor probe.
The instruction to apply TLG paste is not applicable for rental units.
Step 12 requires threading the sensor probe's G3/4" port into the extension tube assembly.
warnings_notes: Not applicable for rentals.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step assembly instructions and diagrams for installing the SR-Teletube sensor probe with both -TT-CAB and -TT-TR electronics configurations, including critical wiring guidelines.
chunks.extracted_text:
SR-Teletube Assembly Guide
SR-TT-AG-2412
rheonics

A. For -TT-CAB
  Pass the cable through the CG-TT Cable Gland, add PTFE tape to the thread, and thread it in the AB-TT NPT 1/2" port. Your probe is now ready.
  Once assembly is installed, connect the sensor cable to the appropriate electronics unit (SME-XXX). Refer to the SME wiring guidelines to connect the sensor cable wires to the appropriate terminal block in the Transmitter.

13
B. For -TT-TR
  Wire the sensor cable in the SME-TRD or -TR electronics, add PTFE tape to the 1/2" NPT thread, and thread the sensor probe to the SME.
  Refer to the SME wiring guidelines to connect the sensor cable wires to the appropriate terminal block in the Transmitter.

10
key_facts: The SR-Teletube assembly guide is identified as SR-TT-AG-2412.
For -TT-CAB assembly, the cable is passed through a CG-TT Cable Gland, PTFE tape is added, and it's threaded into an AB-TT NPT 1/2" port.
After installation, the sensor cable must be connected to the appropriate electronics unit (SME-XXX) and its terminal block in the Transmitter.
For -TT-TR assembly, the sensor cable is wired into SME-TRD or -TR electronics, PTFE tape is added to a 1/2" NPT thread, and the sensor probe is threaded to the SME.
Proper wiring requires adherence to SME wiring guidelines to connect sensor cable wires to the Transmitter's terminal block.
warnings_notes: Refer to the SME wiring guidelines to connect the sensor cable wires to the appropriate terminal block in the Transmitter.
